purpose: Allowlist of known Microsoft first-party application IDs (AppIds) and owner-tenant GUIDs. Consumed by ENTRA-ENTAPP-020 (Foreign Apps Impersonating Microsoft Names) to avoid false-positives on legitimate Microsoft-published service principals. Covers the case where Microsoft adds new owner tenants — the AppId-based allowlist is more stable than tenant-GUID matching alone.

ownerTenantIds.name: Microsoft Services
ownerTenantIds.purpose: Most M365 / Office service principals are published from this tenant. The historically-canonical Microsoft owner tenant.

ownerTenantIds.name: Microsoft Corp
ownerTenantIds.purpose: Microsoft's primary corporate tenant. Some developer tools and MSAL test apps publish from here.

ownerTenantIds.name: Microsoft (Defender / security narrower)
ownerTenantIds.purpose: Some Defender-flavored and security-product service principals appear under this owner tenant.

ownerTenantIds.name: Microsoft Graph Command Line Tools
ownerTenantIds.purpose: Dedicated tenant for the Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK / Graph CLI tooling. The Connect-MgGraph SP appears here.

appIds.name: Microsoft Graph Command Line Tools
appIds.purpose: The service principal automatically registered when a tenant runs Connect-MgGraph (PowerShell) for the first time. Most common false-positive in tenants using the Graph PowerShell SDK.

appIds.name: Microsoft Graph
appIds.purpose: The Microsoft Graph API resource itself. Always present in any Entra-using tenant.

appIds.name: Azure Active Directory Graph (legacy)
appIds.purpose: Legacy Azure AD Graph API resource. Deprecated but still present in older tenants.

appIds.name: Microsoft Office
appIds.purpose: The Office client suite SP. Present in any tenant using Office desktop / web clients.

appIds.name: Office 365 SharePoint Online
appIds.purpose: SharePoint Online resource. Present in any SharePoint-using tenant.

appIds.name: Office 365 Exchange Online
appIds.purpose: Exchange Online resource. Present in any Exchange-using tenant.

appIds.name: Microsoft Exchange REST API Based Powershell
appIds.purpose: Modern EXO PowerShell module — Connect-ExchangeOnline registers this.

appIds.name: Microsoft Azure PowerShell
appIds.purpose: Az PowerShell module SP. Present in tenants using Az or AzureRM.

appIds.name: Microsoft Azure CLI
appIds.purpose: az CLI client SP. Present in tenants using Azure CLI.

calibrationNote: v1 list compiled from empirical observation (M365-Assess lab tenants) + community-documented Microsoft well-known AppIds. Microsoft maintains many more first-party apps than this; expand the list as new false-positives surface in customer reports. AppId is more stable than ownerTenantId because Microsoft can move an app to a new tenant — the AppId stays the same.
